text:
Market today: Mixed but tilted cautious for tech/chips. Morgan Stanley's Wilson flagging the chip trade as 'resembling silver' (a crowded, momentum-driven commodity play prone to sharp reversals) is the real driver today — that's a direct shot at your book.

Your holdings: AVGO is the urgent one — down 15.7%, your largest position, on margin. That pain compounds fast. PLTR is down 9.2% and scanner says SELL — not catastrophic yet but stop at $85 gives you room you may not want to keep giving. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 15.7% — decay and leverage are eating it daily; this one needs a decision, not a watch.

Watch: 1) Whether the Morgan Stanley chip warning gains traction and starts pulling SMH (your only chip green) back toward its $540 stop. 2) SpaceX Nasdaq-100 inclusion — could lift SPCX and SPAL short-term, but SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO; margin amplifies any reversal hard.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is doing nothing — AVGO and SPAL on margin demand a size or stop decision now.
